research_basis: Recent LLM routing work treats model selection as a cost, latency, quality, and policy tradeoff rather than hard-coding one provider.
OpenAI Agents SDK tracing and guardrail patterns support application-owned orchestration, validation, and reviewable events.
LangGraph workflow guidance favors deterministic code paths for predictable workflows and dynamic agents only where needed.
LLM-as-judge research and practice highlight calibration, confidence, bias tracking, human corrections, and objective outcome precedence.
CornerStone local verification keeps local_test deterministic and forbids model output from being the scenario PASS judge.

scenario_results.notes: Provider switch leaves durable namespaces, wiki, evidence, ontology, missions, agents, workflows, policy, audit, experience, judge records, and promotion ladder intact.
Routing decision records workspace policy, sensitivity, mission type, cost, latency, capability, historical outcome quality, and owner preference.
Allowed local override succeeds; forbidden external/restricted override is denied with policy reason and resolution.
Brain Performance Ledger records provider/model, task, policy, sensitivity, cost, latency, judge quality, reliability, outcomes, corrections, success, and routing influence.
Static model capability registry provides safe local_test baseline when local history is empty.
Brain performance learning stays namespace-local; cross-namespace aggregation is denied without opt-in and allowed only with opt-in governance.
High-risk/high-value route triggers multi-brain contribution records without real provider calls.
Routine route uses a single policy-routed brain and records that ensemble is not default.
Ambiguous outcome judge record includes rubric, evidence, confidence, limitations, and supporting-not-PASS-judge status.
Objective evidence overrides judge opinion while retaining judge as evaluation artifact.
Owner acceptance grounds final success when objective truth is unavailable and judge remains supporting evidence.
Judge recommendation creates a governed candidate lesson, not approved memory or a global rule.
Disagreement adjudication uses evidence, policy, mission goals, prior performance, objective outcomes, and rubric, preserving dissent.
High-risk unresolved disagreement creates an escalation card for the namespace owner instead of proceeding silently.
Calibration report tracks disagreements, reversals, owner overrides, calibration issues, and model-specific bias signals.
Provider routing is auditable with policy, sensitivity, cost/latency, capability, local performance, and owner preference factors.
Evidence bundle and mission remain readable before and after provider switch.
Brain Performance Ledger entries are isolated per namespace, with opt-in aggregation only.
Workspace policy controls model routing and disallows forbidden provider overrides.
Provider swap does not break evidence or durable mission records.
LLM judge cannot become unquestionable authority; objective outcomes and owner acceptance remain stronger signals.

policy_decision.reason: Brain performance learning is namespace-local first; cross-namespace aggregation requires explicit opt-in and governance.
resolution_path: Collect owner/admin opt-in and record aggregation policy before using source namespace performance data.

errors.message: Brain performance learning is namespace-local first; cross-namespace aggregation requires explicit opt-in and governance.
resolution_path: Collect owner/admin opt-in and record aggregation policy before using source namespace performance data.

policy_decisions.reason: Brain performance learning is namespace-local first; cross-namespace aggregation requires explicit opt-in and governance.
resolution_path: Collect owner/admin opt-in and record aggregation policy before using source namespace performance data.

command: The Alpha evidence anchor needs a policy-aware model routing and judge review.

authority.blocked_reason: Owner approval is required before shared truth or autonomous action use.

claim.statement: The Alpha evidence anchor needs a policy-aware model routing and judge review.

command: deterministic validator failed required evidence coverage

judge_conflict.objective_evidence: deterministic validator failed required evidence coverage

command: Create a candidate lesson to require objective validator checks before mission success.

judge_recommendation.recommendation: Create a candidate lesson to require objective validator checks before mission success.

command: Evaluate whether the Alpha evidence brief is decision-ready

limitations: LLM judge is scalable for ambiguous quality dimensions but can be biased and overconfident.
judge_record.subject: Evaluate whether the Alpha evidence brief is decision-ready

command: Evaluate model routing, judging, and disagreement without external provider calls

may_do: run allowed low-risk internal actions in Autopilot mode

escalation_rules: Escalate high-risk, destructive, sensitive, cross-namespace, or external writeback actions.
Escalate when evidence coverage is insufficient or policy decision is deny.
evidence_expectations: Evidence Bundle with at least one artifact reference.
Dry-run diff and expected impact before side-effecting action.
Audit refs for every policy and action transition.
mission.goal: Evaluate model routing, judging, and disagreement without external provider calls
mission.review_cadence: owner review required before high-risk execution; after-action audit after every run

source_claim.statement: The Alpha evidence anchor needs a policy-aware model routing and judge review.

stop_conditions: Policy denies scope, egress, connector capability, data sensitivity, or workspace mode.
A requested action is outside the mission contract.
success_criteria: Use attached evidence refs for every durable claim or action.
Record every action proposal, dry-run, policy decision, approval, result, and audit event.

errors.message: Requested model override violates workspace policy, sensitivity, cost, latency, or egress limits.
resolution_path: Use an allowed local provider, lower sensitivity through policy review, or request explicit admin approval.

policy_decisions.reason: Requested model override violates workspace policy, sensitivity, cost, latency, or egress limits.
resolution_path: Use an allowed local provider, lower sensitivity through policy review, or request explicit admin approval.
